verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass TM protein predominantly resident in the ER membrane, with its N-terminal EF-hand/SAM domain facing the ER lumen (equivalent to extracellular space in the PM orientation). A documented minority plasma membrane pool (STIM1PM) exists with the N-terminal domain facing the extracellular environment, accessible to antibodies. Additionally, upon Ca²⁺ store depletion, STIM1 translocates to ER-PM junctions and accumulates near/at the plasma membrane. The dominant compartment is ER, but a real surface-accessible pool exists both constitutively (STIM1PM) and upon store-depletion activation, qualifying as dual_localization/contextual.
